\setcounter{section}{5}
\section{Tables of Zeros of Quadratic Dirichlet \texorpdfstring{$L$}{L}-Functions of Small Conductor}

All zeros tabulated in Appendix~F were computed to 70 decimal places and rigorously certified using the methodology of Appendix~A of the main paper.  Working precision was at least 1500~bits.  The following table summarizes the certified upper bounds:

\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}

\setlength{\LTright}{\fill}

$\chi_3$    & $\Q(\sqrt{3})$    & 1043 & $< 5\times 10^{-419}$ \\
$\chi_5$    & $\Q(\sqrt{5})$    & 1004 & $< 3\times 10^{-428}$ \\

\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_2)$}{L(s,chi2)} (Conductor 8, \texorpdfstring{$d = 2$}{d=2})}
\label{subsec:chi2}

The first 1016 zeros of $L(s,\chi_2)$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{2})$ is $\Delta_K = 8$, since $2 \equiv 2 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_2(1) = \chi_2(7) = +1$, $\chi_2(3) = \chi_2(5) = -1$, with $\chi_2(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,8) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_2)|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_3)$}{L(s,chi3)} (Conductor 12, \texorpdfstring{$d = 3$}{d=3})}
\label{subsec:chi3}

The first 1043 zeros of $L(s,\chi_3)$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{3})$ is $\Delta_K = 12$, since $3 \equiv 3 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_3(1) = \chi_3(11) = +1$, $\chi_3(5) = \chi_3(7) = -1$, with $\chi_3(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,12) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_3)|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

%% ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_5)$}{L(s,chi5)} (Conductor 5, \texorpdfstring{$d = 5$}{d=5})}
\label{subsec:chi5}

The first 1004 zeros of $L(s,\chi_5)$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{5})$ is $\Delta_K = 5$, since $5 \equiv 1 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_5(1) = \chi_5(4) = +1$, $\chi_5(2) = \chi_5(3) = -1$, with $\chi_5(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,5) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_5)|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

\setlength{\LTright}{\fill}

%% ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_6)$}{L(s,chi6)} (Conductor 24, \texorpdfstring{$d = 6$}{d=6})}
\label{subsec:chi6}

The first 1039 zeros of $L(s,\chi_6)$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{6})$ is $\Delta_K = 24$, since $6 \equiv 2 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_6(1,5,19,23) = +1$, $\chi_6(7,11,13,17) = -1$, with $\chi_6(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,24) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_6)|$ appear in the rightmost column.

\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_7)$}{L(s,chi7)} (Conductor 28, \texorpdfstring{$d = 7$}{d=7})}
\label{subsec:chi7}

The first 1044 zeros of $L(s,\chi_7)$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{7})$ is $\Delta_K = 28$, since $7 \equiv 3 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_7(1,3,9,19,25,27) = +1$, $\chi_7(5,11,13,15,17,23) = -1$, with $\chi_7(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,28) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_7)|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

%% ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_{10})$}{L(s,chi10)} (Conductor 40, \texorpdfstring{$d = 10$}{d=10})}
\label{subsec:chi10}

The first 1040 zeros of $L(s,\chi_{10})$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{10})$ is $\Delta_K = 40$, since $10 \equiv 2 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_{10}(1,3,9,13,27,31,37,39) = +1$, $\chi_{10}(7,11,17,19,21,23,29,33) = -1$, with $\chi_{10}(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,40) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_{10})|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

\setlength{\LTright}{\fill}

\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_{11})$}{L(s,chi11)} (Conductor 44, \texorpdfstring{$d = 11$}{d=11})}
\label{subsec:chi11}

The first 1038 zeros of $L(s,\chi_{11})$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{11})$ is $\Delta_K = 44$, since $11 \equiv 3 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_{11}(1,5,7,9,19,25,35,37,39,43) = +1$, $\chi_{11}(3,13,15,17,21,23,27,29,31,41) = -1$, with $\chi_{11}(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,44) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_{11})|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}

%% ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
\subsection{Zeros of \texorpdfstring{$L(s,\chi_{13})$}{L(s,chi13)} (Conductor 13, \texorpdfstring{$d = 13$}{d=13})}
\label{subsec:chi13}

The first 1020 zeros of $L(s,\chi_{13})$ are given here to 70 decimal places.  The fundamental discriminant of $\Q(\sqrt{13})$ is $\Delta_K = 13$, since $13 \equiv 1 \pmod{4}$.  The character satisfies $\chi_{13}(1,3,4,9,10,12) = +1$, $\chi_{13}(2,5,6,7,8,11) = -1$, with $\chi_{13}(n) = 0$ for $\gcd(n,13) > 1$.  Certified upper bounds on $|L(\tfrac{1}{2}+i\gamma_k',\chi_{13})|$ appear in the rightmost column.
\hfill{\small\hyperref[sec:quicklinks]{[\,back to quick links\,]}}
